title: 1.1: Shows evidence that the development server was started (e.g., server startup output or URL binding)
1.2: Shows evidence of running tests with Jest output (e.g., 'PASS', test count, exit code)
1.3: States that test-only globals include items like TextEncoder, TextDecoder, fetch, and matchMedia which are set up in the test framework before tests execute.
1.4: States that environment variables like TZ=UTC are set only when tests run (e.g., configured in the test-client script)
1.5: States that polyfills like ResizeObserver are installed only during tests (e.g., from resize-observer-polyfill package)
1.6: Describes network blocking behavior during tests, including how unmocked requests fail (e.g., nock.disableNetConnect() causing ENETUNREACH errors)
1.7: Shows a concrete API mock example using nock with URL patterns and response data (e.g., using useNock helper with nock().get().reply(403, {error: 'authorization_required'}))
1.8: Traces the data flow from mocked API response through the action creator to the test assertion (e.g., request action dispatched, nock intercepts, failure action dispatched with error)
1.9: States that tests mock the config module (e.g., @automattic/calypso-config) using jest.mock() to control feature flag values
1.10: Reports concrete evidence of configuration differences between test and development environments (e.g., env_id values differ, or 97 feature flags differ)
2.1: Claims that tests run in a jsdom browser simulation environment
